name: feat-ESE-19-1
title: Architecture: 2D vs 3D physics selection and collider design
description: Write architecture document for 2D GPU physics. Must cover: GpuBody2D vs GpuBody3D tag-based selection, GpuCircleCollider and GpuWorldBoundary as entities, separate 2D spatial hash grid (NxN, 9-neighbor search), field-level binding using fields(Transform, px, py), and how world boundary entities participate in the narrowphase.
acceptanceCriteria:
- Document covers 2D/3D selection via tags, collider types, 2D grid design, Transform field binding, world boundary collision algorithm
- Diagram showing 2D physics pipeline (clear grid, populate, collide circles, collide boundaries, integrate)
- Explains how world boundary entities are stored and queried in the narrowphase
demoDeliverable: Walk through the architecture document. Show the 2D pipeline diagram and explain world boundary collision.
testingScenarios: - Review checklist: all sections present, no ambiguity on 2D/3D selection
stakeholderUnderstanding: Defines how 2D physics coexists with 3D. Tags select which pipeline processes each entity. World boundaries are entities with a normal+distance collider. The 2D grid is simpler (NxN, 9 neighbors vs 27). Field selection means we only bind px,py keeping within buffer limits.
